content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Subgoal Prompting for Enhanced Mathematical Problem Solving in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Current prompting methods for LLMs in math problem solving often rely on static or model-generated chains of thought, which can miss key intermediate steps and lead to brittle or verbose reasoning. There is a need for prompting strategies that actively adapt to problem structure and guide the model through critical subgoals to improve accuracy and robustness.",
  "motivation": "LLMs frequently falter on multi-step math problems due to overlooked intermediate reasoning or compounding errors. Unlike existing approaches, dynamically identifying and explicitly prompting for human-relevant subgoals may enable more reliable decomposition and verification of reasoning on-the-fly. As LLMs are increasingly deployed in STEM settings, improved prompting methods are crucial for trustworthy, modular mathematical reasoning.",
  "method": "We propose Dynamic Subgoal Prompting (DSP): an external agent analyzes a math problem and injects explicit, context-aware subgoal prompts at identified logical junctures. The agent uses a lightweight classifier (fine-tuned on solution graphs) to parse the problem, segment it into minimal critical subproblems, and iteratively prompts the LLM to solve and verify each in sequence. DSP adaptively refines subgoals based on model uncertainty and solution traces, encouraging modular, error-localized reasoning without excessive verbosity.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate DSP on GSM8K and MATH datasets. Baselines include standard Chain-of-Thought, MathPrompter, and self-correction prompting. Metrics will assess final answer accuracy, intermediate subgoal correctness, and solution trace length. Ablations will test the necessity of dynamic subgoal segmentation vs. static decomposition."
}
